title: sambucus racemosa subsp. sieboldiana
intro: Sambucus racemosa subsp. sieboldiana, also known as the Japanese elder, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Adoxaceae, native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa subsp. sieboldiana include relief from cold and flu symptoms, improved immune function, and reduced inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make medicinal products such as cough syrups, expectorants, and topical creams.
